Neumiller & Beardslee 
ATTORNEYS AND COUNSELORS 
ROBERT L. BEARDSLEE 
BENJAMIN F. GREEN 
L. NEUMILLER 
THOMAS J. SHEPHARD 
DONALD W. WEST 
DUNCAN R. McPHERSON 
JAMES D. SELLARS 
RUDY V. BI LAWSKI 
ROBERT C. MORRISON 
JAMES R. DYKE 
ARTHUR COFOD 
.JAMES A. ASKEW 
JOHN P. BYRD 
FRANK E. AUSTIN 
TIMOTHY H. B. YARYAN 
GORDON E. HEPLER 
RALPH C. OGDEN III 
MARC H. GREENBERG 
STEVEN A. HERUM 
CARTER P. HOLLY 
LESLEY D. HOLLAND 
400 FIRST FEDERAL PLAZA BUILDING 
6 SOUTH EL DORADO STREET 
P. O. DRAWER 20 
STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95201 
(209) 948-8200 
GEORGE A. DITZ 
1889-1971 
.JOHN W. STOVALL 
NELSON E. BAHLER 
KENNETH R. LEvY 
MR. J. RICHARD EICH~~N 
Accounting Specialist 
State of California 
March 26, 1980 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Dear Mr. Eichman: 
I want to thank you for your gracious assistance 
in connection with my questions concerning lobbying and 
periodic reporting. 
Your guidance in this matter was 
helpful and appreciated. 
IRVING L. NEUMILLER 
1899-1970 
2936-4723 
This letter is merely to confirm our conversation 
that individual attorneys in a law firm do not accumulate 
their time spent influencing legislative or administrative 
action, pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section 
18239(e) (1) and (2), to determine whether the reporting 
requirements of a lobbyist have been activated with respect 
to a law firm. 
From our conversation, I understand the Act 
to focus upon the activities of an individual attorney to 
determine whether or not that attorney has satisfied either 
the compensation test or the time test. 
The Act does not 
contemplate that a law firm would aggegate the time spent or 
entitled compensation of individual attorneys in order to 
determine whether or not the law firm satisfies the threshold 
requirements of 2 Cal. Ad. Code § 18239(e) (1) or (2). 
If it would not be inconvenient, I would appreciate 
a short written opinion in this regard. 
Again, thank you 
for your assistance in this matter. 
SAH:jlg 
Very truly yours, 
Suwt~~ 
STEVEN A. HERDrvt 
for 
NEUMILLER & BEARDSLEE

sections.question: Do individual attorneys in a law firm accumulate their time spent influencing legislative or administrative action to determine whether the reporting requirements of a lobbyist have been activated with respect to a law firm, pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section 18239(e)(1) and (2)?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether individual attorneys' time spent on legislative or administrative influence activities should be aggregated at the law firm level to determine if lobbyist reporting requirements are triggered under California Administrative Code Section 18239(e).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The attorney references a prior helpful conversation and requests written confirmation of the guidance received. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question is embedded in the body text. Document dated March 26, 1980.

embedding.summary: A law firm attorney requests written confirmation from the FPPC regarding their prior telephone conversation about whether attorney time spent on lobbying activities should be aggregated at the firm level or evaluated individually to determine lobbyist registration requirements.
